observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended and Management concurred to the following:
3.8.1 Submit justification on the noted deviation of Items 4.3 and 4.4 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-01, on the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund of the LGU;
If not justified, replenish from the General Fund the expenditures inappropriately charged against the LDRRMF; and
Henceforth, strictly utilize the LDRRMF for projects and activities enumerated in Section 5 of the NDRRM-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

Recommendations:
4.10	We recommended and Management is amenable to update its existing policy, for the determination of wage rate of Job Order/Contract of Service personnel and establish guidelines to adhere to the prescribed standards on labor practices.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
1. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit all the required reports on time to the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Accountant to submit to the audit team on the prescribed time for immediate validation and verification;
2.Augment the workforce and personnel in the Accounting Office so that required reports are submitted immediately; and
3. Plan ahead the schedule of conduct of physical count of all properties on date/s nearing yearend to give ample time for the reconciliation of records and avoid delay in the submission thereof.
observations.managementAction:
Personnel from Treasury had been augmented and this matter was already addressed.

Reports are already submitted within the following month.

The Inventory Committee already conducted the semestral physical count. Reports are yet prepared.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Follow through the titling of all its land properties to strengthen claim of ownership over subject properties and ensure that all future acquisitions of land properties be properly supported with Certificates of Title; and
2. Work closely with the PENRO to expedite the release of FLA, and ensure compliance with the same for future similar developments.

We recommended and the Management acceded to:

1. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit all the required reports on time to the Municipal Accountant, and the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team on the prescribed time for immediate validation and verification;

2. Augment the workforce and personnel in the Accounting Office so that required reports are submitted immediately; and 

3. Plan ahead the schedule of conduct of physical count of all properties on date/s nearing yearend to give ample time for the reconciliation of records and avoid delay in the submission thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to evaluate its existing policy, if any, for the determination of wage rate of Job Order personnel and establish guidelines to adhere to the minimum standards on labor practices.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the guidelines set by BIR Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 which implements Republic Act No. 8424 and BIR RMC No. 23-2012 for withholding of taxes and to remit on time taxes withheld to avoid penalties for non-compliance thereof.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Disbursing Officer to:

Submit to this office their separate cashbooks on their respective cash disbursements.
Henceforth, strictly adhere with the existing regulations on the proper preparation and maintenance of cashbook for timely validation and verification of their accountabilities.
We recommended that Management submit the Narrative Reports on trip undertaken of personnel listed in Annex A for our evaluation.

We recommended that Management expedite the filling in of the vacant positions for more effective administration of those offices and to be able to deliver the basic services needed by the LGU’s constituents efficiently and effectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries to correct the misstatements of the affected accounts. Henceforth, ensure that costs of major repairs, rehabilitation, improvement and construction of infrastructure assets should be capitalized or recognized as property, plant and equipment in accordance with International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 17.

We recommended and Management agreed to review the MOA and communicate with the source agency on the disposition of the unused funds in the Trust Fund, either to be returned to the source agency or reverted back to the general fund, to close the dormant accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of supporting documents on the monthly claims of salaries and wages of Officers, Employees, and JO personnel. In addition, we are requiring the management to submit the lacking documents relating to the payment of salaries and wages to officers, employees and JO personnel.

We recommended that Management :

a. Refrain from signing loan applications if the mandated net take home pay of the employee is below P5,00 - ; and

b. Ensure that the net take home pay of all employees does not fall below P5,00 -  by strictly following the order of preference concerning authorized deductions provided for under Section 55 of the General Provisions of the GAA.

We recommend the management to:

a.	Require the SB Secretary to furnish the Accounting Office copies of the monthly SB resolution/minutes and the later then include the said reports to support the monthly claims of the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) members;

b.	Ensure the completeness of supporting documents, through the Municipal Accountant, on the monthly claims of salaries, PERA and RATA of the SB Members; and

c.	Submit the lacking documents relating to the SB Personnel Benefits.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Prepare and submit the request for write-off of dormant receivables from SEA-K loans, together with the complete set of documentary requirements; and
2. Prepare and submit the request for write-off of dormant accounts recorded under the Other Receivables in the Trust Fund after conducting a verification, analysis and validation of the existence of said receivables.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

1. Instruct the BAC Secretariat to ensure that contracts are fully supported with the required documents before contract signing; and

2. Ensure the prompt submission of copies of perfected contracts along with the required supporting documents, for timely review by the Audit Team.

We recommended Management to:

1. Reconcile Budget and FS data on the total funds available for DRRMF CY 2021;

2. Provide justification for the following:
a. Charges to DRRMF that do not directly relate to DRRM activities
b. Overspent items and expenditures without budget
c. Non-implementation of other PPAs
d. Charges made prior to approval of the corresponding Budget

3. Exclude items not directly related to DRRM in the preparation of subsequent LDRRMFIP and source these items instead from the General Fund pursuant to the JMC No. 2014-1;

4. Ensure that all charges to the DRRMF are made within and/or duly supported with an approved Budget, and must be made only after such approval; and

5. Strictly monitor the implementation of all planned DRRM PPAs to ensure achievement of the LGU’s goals in mitigating the impact of disasters/calamities.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare and submit the request for write-off of dormant receivables from SEA-K loans, together with the complete set of documentary requirements; and
Prepare and submit the request for write-off of dormant accounts recorded under the Other Receivables in the Trust Fund after conducting a verification, analysis and validation of the existence of said receivables.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Follow through the titling of all its land properties to strengthen claim of ownership over subject properties and ensure that all future acquisitions of land properties be properly supported with Certificates of Title; and
Work closely with the PENRO to expedite the release of FLA, and ensure compliance with the same for future similar developments.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Instruct the BAC Secretariat to ensure that contracts are fully supported with the required documents before contract signing; and
Ensure the prompt submission of copies of perfected contracts along with the required supporting documents, for timely review by the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit all the required reports on time to the Municipal Accountant, and the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team on the prescribed time for immediate validation and verification;
Augment the workforce and personnel in the Accounting Office so that required reports are submitted immediately; and
Plan ahead the schedule of conduct of physical count of all properties on date/s nearing yearend to give ample time for the reconciliation of records and avoid delay in the submission thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to evaluate its existing policy, if any, for the determination of wage rate of Job Order personnel and establish guidelines to adhere to the minimum standards on labor practices.

We recommended Management to:
Reconcile Budget and FS data on the total funds available for DRRMF CY 2021;
Provide justification for the following:
Charges to DRRMF that do not directly relate to DRRM
Activities
Overspent items and expenditures without budget
Non-implementation of other PPAs
Charges made prior to approval of the corresponding Budget
Exclude items not directly related to DRRM in the preparation of subsequent LDRRMFIP and source these items instead from the General Fund pursuant to the JMC No. 2014-1;
Ensure that all charges to the DRRMF are made within and/or duly supported with an approved Budget, and must be made only after such approval; and
Strictly monitor the implementation of all planned DRRM PPAs to ensure achievement of the LGU’s goals in mitigating the impact of disasters/calamities.

We recommended that Management:

1. Ensure the preparation of bank reconciliation statements at year-end and the recording of the corresponding reconciling items to arrive at adjusted Cash in Bank LCCA balances before the submission of financial statements; and

2. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer conduct reconciliation of the GL and Cashbook balances to ensure completeness and accuracy of the Cash in Bank – LCCA account at year-end.

We recommended that Management:

1. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct the annual physical count of all properties of the LGU, pursuant to the aforementioned provisions of the Manual, and ensure that these are reconciled with the General Ledger before the financial statements are submitted for audit.

2. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain and update the property and stock cards, and require the renewal of PARs thereof.

observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II, Item 2.

3. Strictly observe the guidelines in the allocation and utilization of the LDRRMF pursuant to NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 
1. Instruct the Accounting Office to avoid using the Petty Cash Fund to pay for regular and recurring expenses. 
2. Comply with the provisions of Section 4 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 as to the documentary requirements for its disbursements, in order to fully establish the validity of the claims to the fund.

Liquidation reports were already submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
1. Establish clear-cut guidelines for the grant of communication allowances to officials and employees; and
2. Consider adopting the post-paid plans offered by the network providers for economic reasons and to foster equity in the grant of allowances in relation to actual communication needs of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: An EO has been issued to address this matter.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
1. Should limit the annual appropriation and disbursement of funds for extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses to the 2% limitation/ceiling derived from the basic property tax receipts in the next preceding calendar year, as prescribed under Section 325(h) of R.A. 7160; and
2. Ensure that all claims are supported by receipts and other necessary supporting documents depending on the nature of the expense charged in order to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances.

We recommended that Management:
1. Stop the practice of incorporating expenditures in the LDRRMF Plan which are not directly related to the implementation of DRRM Programs. Budgetary requirements for the Personnel Services, Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses and Capital Outlay of the DRRM Office should be sourced from the General Fund of the LGU pursuant to Section 4.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014; and
2. Strictly observe the guidelines in the allocation and utilization of the LDRRMF pursuant to NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Total expenses not directly related to DRRMS PPAs has decreased from previous year.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 4.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to submit copies of perfected contracts along with the required relevant supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution/approval so that timely review by the team will performed and deficiencies will be corrected.
observations.reason: There is already progress in the prompt submission of perfected contracts for auditorial review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management should settle the legal issues of the unauthorized reclamation project in line with the established regulations to avoid the penalties of pursuing an illegal project.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had already applied for foreshore lease last year (following NEDA's recommendation that reclamation not necessary) and is now just awaiting approval.
observations.reason: Amidst uncertainties in the transfer of management of the Mantigue Island Nature Park, the LGU will still continue processing the Foreshore Lease Application with the PENRO. All other documentary requirements were duly submitted and still awaiting action of the same office.

We recommended that Management:
Establish clear-cut guidelines for the grant of communication allowances to officials and employees; and
Consider adopting the post-paid plans offered by the network providers for economic reasons and to foster equity in the grant of allowances in relation to actual communication needs of the LGU.

We recommended that Management:
Should limit the annual appropriation and disbursement of funds for extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses to the 2% limitation/ceiling derived from the basic property tax receipts in the next preceding calendar year, as prescribed under Section 325(h) of R.A. 7160; and
Ensure that all claims are supported by receipts and other necessary supporting documents depending on the nature of the expense charged in order to avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances.

We recommended that Management:
1. Stop the practice of incorporating expenditures in the LDRRMF Plan which are not directly related to the implementation of DRRM Programs. Budgetary requirements for the Personnel Services, Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses and Capital Outlay of the DRRM Office should be sourced from the General Fund of the LGU pursuant to Section 4.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014; and
2. Strictly observe the guidelines in the allocation and utilization of the LDRRMF pursuant to NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to adhere to the required number of observers in the procurement activities and ensure that only qualified observers are invited pursuant to Section 13.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that Management direct the Bids and Awards Committee to observe the timeline set under the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the evaluation, post qualification, issuance of NOA, execution of contract and issuance of NTP to expedite the implementation of the project.

We recommended that the municipality should (1) prepare an LDRRMFIP for the DRRM Program following the prescribed format and furnish the DILG - Local Government Operations Office; (2) incorporate the prior years’ unspent LDRRM Funds; (3) fully implement all the programs, activities and projects (P/A/Ps) included in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that Management through the Accounting Office strictly implement the withholding of retention money or secure a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent (1%) but not to exceed five percent (5%) of the total contract price on the procurement of goods to protect the municipality from any manufacturing defects and assure performance of warranty obligation, in compliance with Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 as amended by GPPB Resolution No. 30-2017.

We recommended that Management through the Accounting Office strictly implement the withholding of retention money or secure a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent (1%) but not to exceed five percent (5%) of the total contract price on the procurement of goods to protect the municipality from any manufacturing defects and assure performance of warranty obligation, in compliance with Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 as amended by GPPB Resolution No. 30-2017.

Seek the original journal entry regarding the negative balance in the subsidiary ledger for Bid Docs and input the necessary correcting entries; and

We recommended that the Management submit the approved Request for Extension of Contract Time with a written consent from the Guarantor Veterans Bank.

Based on the quoted clause of the bank guarantee, any change in the contract, particularly the request of the Contractor, Footprints Construction, for Extension of Contract Time or Variation Order, shall in effect release them from liability even in absence of notice. Thus, the need to get a written consent from Veterans Bank, guarantor.

We recommended the Management to facilitate the titling of the Land Properties to strengthen claim on ownership over subject properties.
observations.reason: The LGU is still processing documents for the Transfer Certificates of Title in order to address this finding and also to secure the LGU on its ownership of the lands.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the guidelines set by the Bureau of Internal Revenue and to submit sufficient proof of the tax exemption of CAMELCO and CAMTECO and to consistently apply the proper tax rates for GMPs in order avoid the issuance of Notices of Charge and penalties as provided by the law.

For findings #1 and #2, we recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Record sales and service fees in a proper income account instead of Other Payables;
Seek the original journal entry regardingthe negative balance in the subsidiary ledger for Bid Docs and input the necessary correcting entries; and
Exhaust all necessary remedies to substantiate the unreconciled balance and remit the necessary tax dues.
We recommended that the Management submit the approved Request for Extension of Contract Time with a written consent from the Guarantor Veterans Bank.

We recommended that the Management should settle the legal issues of the unauthorized reclamation project in line with the established regulations to avoid the penalties of pursuing an illegal project.
We enjoined Management to advise the Municipal School Board to exercise due prudence in accommodating expenses charged against the Special Education Fund.
We recommended Management to require the Accountable Officer to refund or return any excess cash advances.
We recommended the Management to prohibit release of cash before securing the necessary documentary requirements and vouchers.
We recommended the Management to facilitate the titling of the Land Properties to strengthen claim on ownership over subject properties.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the guidelines set by the Bureau of Internal Revenue and to submit sufficient proof of the tax exemption of CAMELCO and CAMTECO and to consistently apply the proper tax rates for GMPs in order avoid the issuance of Notices of Charge and penalties as provided by the law.

We recommended to Management that submission of the purchase orders, within the prescribed period be strictly complied in order to avoid suspension in audit of transactions covered by the unsubmitted documents. These Purchase Orders should be filled completely to avoid confusion and difficulty in demanding compliance and enforcing penalties. We also recommended management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to send notice of deliveries to the Office of the Auditor on purchases made to attain timely and relevant audit results and to facilitate post audit of transaction.
We recommended to Management that the excess fund be reverted to the General Fund in accordance with the aforementioned Resolution.

We recommended Management, particularly authorized drivers of government vehicles, submit regularly to the audit team within the first ten (10) days of the following month the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels in accordance with existing Rules and Regulations.

We recommended to Management that submission of the purchase orders, within the prescribed period be strictly complied in order to avoid suspension in audit of transactions covered by the unsubmitted documents. These Purchase Orders should be filled completely to avoid confusion and difficulty in demanding compliance and enforcing penalties. We also recommended management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to send notice of deliveries to the Office of the Auditor on purchases made to attain timely and relevant audit results and to facilitate post audit of transaction.
We recommended to Management that the excess fund be reverted to the General Fund in accordance with the aforementioned Resolution.

We recommended Management, particularly authorized drivers of government vehicles, submit regularly to the audit team within the first ten (10) days of the following month the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels in accordance with existing Rules and Regulations.

We recommend that management shall ensure that the projects, programs and activities in the AIP that is funded by the 20% development fund shall be in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1, in order that the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes geared towards the creation of jobs, promotion of economic growth and poverty alleviation shall be achieved.

We recommended to Management that submission of the perfected contract and purchase orders, within the prescribed period be strictly complied in order to avoid suspension in audit of transactions covered by the unsubmitted documents, as well as the penalty prescribed by law under Section 4.1 of the aforementioned Circular which subjects to administrative disciplinary action any unjustified failure of the officials and employees concerned to comply to the requirements.

We recommended to effect an adjustment on the overstatement of RATA through reimbursement of the amount overpaid and that for the ensuing months, RATA shall be pro-rated based on actual work/attendance of the official in accordance with the abovementioned Circular. We also recommend that payment for RATA should be paid separate from the monthly salary; and a Certification by the Secretary of the Sanggunian for their attendance to Session and another Certification by the Human Resource Management Office for No Vehicle Assigned by the Government should be attached.
